home.titleChat: Keep ideas, context, and next actions in one place
home.titleWorkspace: Move the next task forward in {{name}}

planReview.errorTitle: Failed to render the plan review card
planReview.runningHint: This session is implementing the plan now.

planReview.approvedHint: This plan has already been approved.

guide.title: Conversation Onboarding
guide.subtitle: Learn the left project/session area, the composer with commands and @ file mentions, and the right-side panels.
guide.bannerDesc: Spend two minutes on this guide and you will know how projects, sessions, the composer, and the right panel work together.

modeClarify.title: This is Clarify mode
modeClarify.description: Use it to understand the goal, boundaries, constraints, and acceptance criteria before execution. It is better for asking and narrowing things down first.

modeAgent.title: This is Project Execution mode
modeAgent.description: Use it when a task needs a visible loop: plan the next step, use tools, review results, and keep project work moving.

modeCode.title: This is Code mode
modeCode.description: Use it for direct implementation tasks such as editing files, writing code, running checks, and handling engineering details.

modeAcp.title: This is ACP mode
modeAcp.description: Use it as the control tower for complex work: clarify boundaries and acceptance first, then design the architecture, break down tasks, and delegate execution while the main agent stays out of direct coding.

leftSidebar.title: This is the project and session area
leftSidebar.description: Your work is organized here as projects and sessions. Use it to switch projects, revisit old sessions, and search earlier context.

sessionActions.title: These are the project and session quick actions
sessionActions.description: Use this area to create projects, start a session, export conversations, and combine it with right-click actions for rename, pin, duplicate, or delete.

composer.title: This is the composer
composer.description: Most work starts here: describe the task, send messages, attach images, switch models, enable web search, or enter Plan Mode. Enter sends, Shift+Enter adds a newline.

mentions.title: Use @ here to mention files
mentions.description: Type @ inside the composer to search files from the current working folder. This inserts structured file references, which is much better than typing paths manually.

commands.title: Open commands, Skills, and MCP here
commands.description: Click this + button to add Commands, Skills, and Connectors to the current message. Typing / opens slash-command suggestions.

rightPanel.title: This is the execution panel
rightPanel.description: Use it to monitor progress and context: Steps shows what is running, Files shows the tree, Artifacts shows changed files, and Context shows folder, model, tokens, and background commands.

leftSidebar.title: Left: Projects and Sessions
bullets: The narrow far-left rail is app navigation; the wide left panel is where projects and sessions live.
A project works like a workspace and can store its name, working folder, SSH connection, and default model.
Each project can contain many sessions, and sessions support create, rename, pin, delete, and mode switching.
Session search matches both titles and loaded message content, while older sessions collapse into history.
Right-click a project or session to export, duplicate, clear messages, or change folders.

composer.title: Center Bottom: Composer and Send
bullets: The composer supports multiline editing, per-session draft memory, and input history navigation.
Press Enter to send and Shift+Enter for a newline; extra sends during a run go into the queue.
The bottom toolbar controls model selection, web search, Skills, Plan Mode, and image upload.
When the current mode requires a working folder, the composer reminds you before sending.
The wand button rewrites your prompt into a clearer and more actionable task description.

mentions.title: Composer: @ File Mentions
bullets: Type @ in the composer to search files inside the current working folder instead of typing full paths.
Use arrow keys to move, Tab or Enter to insert, and Esc to dismiss the picker.
Inserted files become structured file references, not plain text, so the assistant can use them reliably.
The selected-file bar lets you preview, locate references, remove one file, or clear them all.

commands.title: Composer: / Commands and the + Menu
bullets: Type / at the start of input to open command suggestions backed by local command templates.
The + menu gives visual access to Commands, Skills, and Connectors.
Selecting a Skill adds its capability prefix to the next message, which is great for repeatable workflows.
Ctrl+K opens the global command palette for UI actions; it is different from chat slash commands.

rightPanel.title: Right: Execution and Resource Panels
bullets: Steps is the fastest way to see what the assistant is doing right now and how far it has progressed.
Plan is for planning before implementation, especially for larger or multi-file work.
Files shows the local or SSH file tree, while Artifacts summarizes files created, edited, or deleted in this run.
Team shows multi-agent collaboration, and Context summarizes folder, model, tokens, context window, and background commands.
The right panel can be collapsed, switched by tabs, and resized by dragging.
Some files open an additional preview panel for HTML, Markdown, images, spreadsheets, PDF, and more.

quickStart.title: Recommended First Steps
bullets: Create a project and bind a working folder before starting serious work.
Use Clarify for requirement discovery, Project Execution for traceable project work, and Code for implementation tasks.
For code work, mention files with @ first, then describe the goal and constraints.
During execution, keep an eye on Steps, Files, Artifacts, and Context on the right side.
When the session grows long, use Context compression to keep the model focused.

messageList.startConversationDesc: Ask anything, or use web search and selected MCP tools.
messageList.startClarifyDesc: Interrogate the goal, constraints, risks, and gaps before planning. It defaults to clarification-first behavior, but permissions match Code mode.
messageList.startAgentDesc: Select a working folder, then ask AgentBoard to move the project forward.
messageList.startCodingDesc: Describe what you want to build and the assistant will write the code.
messageList.homeDescAcp: Start ACP mode for architecture confirmation, constraint clarification, and execution breakdown.
messageList.homeDescAutoChat: After you send, it picks a better model automatically based on task complexity.
messageList.homeDescAutoClarify: After you send, it picks a model better suited for questioning and narrowing the plan.
messageList.homeDescAutoAgent: After you send, it picks a model better suited for project execution, file edits, and tools.
messageList.homeDescAutoCode: After you send, it picks a model better suited for coding, refactoring, and analysis.
messageList.homeDescAutoAcp: After you send, it picks a model better suited for architecture confirmation, constraint clarification, and execution breakdown.
messageList.homeDescChatGeneral: Good for everyday chat, writing, and lightweight questions.
messageList.homeDescChatVision: Good for chat and can also respond quickly with image context.
messageList.homeDescClarifyGeneral: Good for follow-up questions, goal shaping, and filling in constraints.
messageList.homeDescClarifyThinking: Better for deep questioning, breaking down problems, and converging on a plan.
messageList.homeDescAgentGeneral: Good for entering project context and moving a task forward with traceable steps.
messageList.homeDescAgentTools: Better for tool-assisted project work: reading files, editing code, and running workflows.
messageList.homeDescCodeGeneral: Good for generating code, writing scripts, and routine development work.
messageList.homeDescCodeVision: Useful for coding and for analyzing issues with screenshots or images.
messageList.homeDescCodeThinking: Better for complex implementation, refactoring, debugging, and multi-step coding tasks.
messageList.tipDropFiles: Tip: Drop files into the input to reference their paths
messageList.writeRegex: Write a regex for email validation
messageList.clarifyIdea: Interrogate this product idea with relentless follow-up questions
messageList.challengeAssumptions: Challenge the hidden assumptions and vague language in this proposal
messageList.exploreRisks: Surface edge cases, failure modes, and second-order consequences
messageList.addErrorHandling: Add error handling to the main module
messageList.reviewCodebase: Review this codebase and suggest improvements
messageList.addTests: Add tests for the main module
messageList.writeTestsExisting: Write tests for the existing code
messageList.imageCopiedDesc: You can paste it into chat or documents

assistantMessage.continueToolExecutionHint: The last run stopped during tool execution. Click to continue in this same message without creating a new AI message.

imageError.descTimeout: The request exceeded 10 minutes. Try simplifying the prompt, reducing image complexity, or retrying.
imageError.descNetwork: Failed to reach the image service. Check your network, proxy, and Base URL settings, then try again.
imageError.descAborted: The request was interrupted before completion. You can retry now.
imageError.descApi: The model service rejected this request. Review the model, API key, and request parameters.
imageError.descUnknown: An unexpected error occurred while generating the image. Please retry.

agentError.descTool: The tool call failed. You can retry or inspect the details below.
agentError.descRuntime: The agent runtime raised an exception. You can try regenerating.
agentError.descAuth: Check your API key, organization, or permission settings.
agentError.descRateLimit: You have been rate limited. Retry later or reduce concurrency.
agentError.descQuota: Your account quota or balance is insufficient. Review billing.
agentError.descTemporaryPause: Repeated upstream failures triggered a short pause. Requests will resume automatically.
agentError.descNetwork: Cannot reach the service. Check network, proxy, and Base URL.
agentError.descTimeout: The request took too long. Retry later or shorten the context.
agentError.descAborted: The request was interrupted before completion. You can retry.
agentError.descServer: The service returned a server-side error. Please retry later.
agentError.descBadRequest: Request was rejected. Check the model, parameters, or messages.
agentError.descUnknown: An unknown error occurred. See details below.
agentError.circuitOpenWithSeconds: Repeated upstream failures temporarily paused retries. Requests should recover automatically in about {{seconds}} seconds.
agentError.circuitOpenWithoutSeconds: Repeated upstream failures temporarily paused retries. Requests should recover automatically soon.
